rationale: The homes display complex multi-plane hip roofs with moderate shadow lengths extending roughly 5–8 ft from the eave edges (per the 50-ft scale bar), consistent with a 6:12 pitch. The ridge lines are well-inset from the eave perimeter but the visible roof plane widths are broad, ruling out anything steeper than 8:12. The triangular hip-end gables show moderate protrusion from overhead, not the dramatic tapering seen on 9:12+ roofs. Regional context (Spring, TX — Houston suburb) strongly favors 6:12 as the standard pitch for upscale suburban hip-roofed homes in this warm climate with no snow-load requirements. The dark shingle texture shows subtle but not dramatic slope definition from the satellite vantage, further corroborating a moderate 6:12 pitch over steeper alternatives.
